page: Amazon Web Services m8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. This x86_64 architecture system features 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Additionally, the server is equipped with 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth in large-block uncached tests. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), contribute to top-tier performance in Passmark extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests. For data processing, the server achieves top-tier results in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression.

While multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are consistently in the top tier, single-core performance represents a relative tradeoff, scoring in the average (middle 50%) range for stress-ng single-core tests. Similarly, LLM text generation for small models falls into the average tier, though prompt processing across small, medium, and large models remains in the top 10%. This resource profile, combining high-density compute, massive local NVMe storage, and high memory bandwidth, is suited for large-scale database operations, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM prompt processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores, powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng and Geekbench, place this instance in the top 10% tier, supported by high-speed DDR5 memory bandwidth and advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. However, single-core performance represents a tradeoff, scoring in the average tier. This server is optimized for high-density multi-threaded workloads, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11,400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal platform combining high-density Intel Xeon compute, fast DDR5 memory, and massive local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors with 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores
Equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Includes 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Achieves top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing
